content: Hey, I'm so excited for my upcoming performance at La Scala in Milan. It's going to be my 10th time performing there.
Wow, that's amazing! La Scala is one of the most famous opera houses in the world. What's your favorite role to perform?

I have to say, I love performing as Don Giovanni. There's something about the music and the character that just speaks to me. By the way, I've been trying to find some time to work on my gardening skills, maybe plant some new flowers in my backyard in Paris.
Don Giovanni is a classic. Gardening is a great hobby, it can be so relaxing. What kind of flowers are you thinking of planting?
I'm not sure yet, maybe some roses or lavender. I've also been trying to learn more about mushroom foraging, have you heard of any good spots near Paris?
Roses and lavender are both beautiful choices. As for mushroom foraging, I've heard that the Fontainebleau forest is a great spot, but you should always be careful and go with an experienced guide.
That sounds like a great idea, I'll have to look into it. I've also been trying to teach my new dog, Willow, some new tricks. She's a golden retriever and she's been keeping me on my toes.
Aww, that's so sweet! Golden retrievers are such great dogs. What kind of tricks are you trying to teach her?
Just the usual, sit, stay, fetch. She's picking it up quickly, but sometimes she gets distracted by all the squirrels in the park.
Ha ha, that's understandable. Squirrels can be pretty entertaining. Do you have any fun plans for after your performance at La Scala?
Actually, I'm thinking of taking a trip to Thailand to learn more about cooking Thai food. I've always loved the cuisine and I think it would be a great experience.
Thai food is amazing, and taking a cooking class would be a great way to learn more about it. Have you traveled to Thailand before?
No, this would be my first time. I'm really looking forward to it, though. I've heard the people and the culture are just wonderful.

That's great! You'll have to try some of the street food while you're there, it's supposed to be incredible. And don't forget to try some of the local markets, they're a great place to find unique souvenirs.
Definitely, I love trying new foods and drinks. And I've also been getting into calligraphy lately, I find it really relaxing.
Calligraphy is a beautiful art form. It's great that you're finding time to relax and pursue your hobbies, especially with your busy schedule as an opera singer.
Yeah, it's not always easy, but it's worth it. I'm thinking of starting a crossword puzzle club with some of my friends, too.
A crossword puzzle club sounds like a great idea! It's always fun to challenge yourself and spend time with friends who share similar interests.
